vision: Turn Birkin from a chat UI with tools into a programmable agent runtime — MCP-compatible, schedulable, observable, capable of executing real work.
ordering_principle: MCP first. Every later item sits on top of the MCP layer.

sprints.name: Phase 1 Debt Cleanup
sprints.goal: Fix review findings that block Phase 2 work

tasks.title: Fix ROADMAP.md truncation
tasks.rationale: ROADMAP.md Phase 4 section is truncated mid-sentence. Must restore.

subtasks.desc: Restore Phase 4 content (deferred monetization, out-of-scope list)
Verify markdown renders correctly end-to-end

acceptance_criteria: Phase 4 section complete with out-of-scope list

tasks.title: Narrow broad exception handlers (10 instances)
tasks.rationale: 10 bare 'except Exception:' blocks mask errors and block observability logging in Phase 2A.

subtasks.desc: Audit all 10 except-Exception sites, determine correct specific exception types
Replace with specific types (ConnectionError, TimeoutError, json.JSONDecodeError, sqlite3.Error, etc.)
Add structured logging with exc_info=True where missing
Add tests for error paths

acceptance_criteria: Zero bare except-Exception in birkin/
All error paths logged with specific type

tasks.title: Add config schema validation (Pydantic)
tasks.rationale: gateway/config.py accepts arbitrary JSON. Invalid state propagates silently.

subtasks.desc: Define BirkinConfig Pydantic model (provider, model, api keys, fallback, system_prompt)
Validate on load and save in config.py
Add migration path for existing birkin_config.json files
Tests: invalid config rejected, valid config round-trips

acceptance_criteria: Invalid config raises ValidationError
Existing configs load without breaking

tasks.title: Add concurrency locks (WikiMemory + Telegram polling)
tasks.rationale: WikiMemory file ops and Telegram global polling state have race conditions.

subtasks.desc: Add threading.Lock to WikiMemory for ingest/delete/update_index
Add asyncio.Lock to Telegram polling start/stop/health_check
Tests: concurrent wiki writes don't corrupt index

acceptance_criteria: No race condition on concurrent wiki ingest
Telegram state transitions are atomic

tasks.title: Add return type annotations to all router functions
tasks.rationale: Missing return types in wiki, settings, workflows routers reduce type safety.

subtasks.desc: Add return type annotations to all endpoint functions
Run mypy or pyright spot-check

acceptance_criteria: All router functions have return type annotations

sprints.name: MCP Foundation
sprints.goal: First-class MCP client/server — Birkin can consume and expose MCP

tasks.title: MCP client: consume external MCP servers
tasks.rationale: Core MCP layer. All Phase 2 features build on this. Tool ABC becomes thin adapter over MCP.

subtasks.desc: Create birkin/mcp/ package structure
Implement MCPClient with stdio and SSE transports
Implement MCPRegistry — discover, connect, list tools from multiple servers
Create MCPToolAdapter: wraps MCP tool as Birkin Tool (bridging Tool ABC)
Wire MCPRegistry into Agent.__init__ so MCP tools appear alongside built-in tools
Config: mcp_servers list in birkin_config.json [{name, command, args, env}]
Tests: mock MCP server, tool discovery, tool execution round-trip

acceptance_criteria: Agent can discover and call tools from an external MCP server
MCP tools show in /api/tools alongside built-ins
stdio and SSE transports both work

tasks.title: MCP server: expose Birkin capabilities as MCP
tasks.rationale: Birkin becomes composable — Claude Code, Cursor, etc. can use Birkin as an MCP server.

subtasks.desc: Implement MCPServer (stdio transport for CLI integration)
Expose built-in tools (shell, file_ops, web_search) as MCP tools
Expose wiki memory as MCP resources (list, read, search)
Expose sessions as MCP resources
Add `birkin mcp serve` CLI command
Tests: connect to Birkin MCP server from test client, call tools, read resources

acceptance_criteria: `birkin mcp serve` starts an MCP server on stdio
External client can list tools and call them
Wiki pages accessible as MCP resources

tasks.title: Skills system (MCP-native)
tasks.rationale: Bundled + community skills as MCP servers. SKILL.md + resources format for discoverability.

subtasks.desc: Define Skill schema: SKILL.md (name, desc, triggers, tools) + resources dir
SkillLoader: scan skills/ directory, parse SKILL.md, register as MCP server
SkillRegistry: list installed skills, enable/disable per session
Create 2 example skills: (1) code-review, (2) web-summarizer
WebUI: skills panel showing installed skills with toggle
Tests: skill discovery, activation, tool routing through skill

acceptance_criteria: Skills loaded from SKILL.md format
Skills register as MCP servers consumed by Agent
Example skills work end-to-end

sprints.name: Execution Engine
sprints.goal: State graph + triggers + cron = workflows that run themselves

tasks.title: State graph execution engine (LangGraph-style)
tasks.rationale: Current BFS executor lacks conditionals, loops, parallel fan-out. State graph is the foundation for all complex workflows.

subtasks.desc: Define StateGraph class: nodes, edges, conditional edges, entry/end points
Implement graph execution with state dict passing between nodes
Add conditional branching (if/else edges based on state)
Add parallel fan-out / fan-in (concurrent node execution)
Add cycle detection with max-iteration guard
Implement checkpoints: save graph state to SQLite, resume from checkpoint
Coexist with existing BFS simple mode (mode: 'simple' | 'graph')
Migrate 2 sample workflows to graph mode as proof
Tests: linear, branching, parallel, loop-with-guard, checkpoint/resume

acceptance_criteria: Graph mode supports conditional edges, parallel fan-out, loops with guard
Checkpoints persist and resume correctly
Existing simple mode workflows unaffected

tasks.title: Trigger abstraction + Cron scheduler
tasks.rationale: Workflows fire on triggers (time, file, webhook, message, custom event). Core automation primitive.

subtasks.desc: Define Trigger ABC: type, config, match(event) -> bool, linked_workflow_id
CronTrigger: APScheduler or asyncio-based cron expressions
FileWatchTrigger: watchdog-based file change detection
WebhookTrigger: generic webhook endpoint that routes to workflows
TriggerScheduler: manages active triggers, starts/stops with app lifespan
API: CRUD endpoints for triggers (/api/triggers)
WebUI: trigger config in workflow editor (cron expression builder)
Tests: cron fires at expected time, file trigger detects change, webhook routes correctly

acceptance_criteria: Cron trigger fires workflows on schedule
File watch trigger detects changes
Webhook trigger routes inbound to correct workflow
Triggers persist across restart

tasks.title: Evaluation framework
tasks.rationale: Fixed question sets + snapshot diff + per-provider cost tracking. Feeds observability dashboard.

subtasks.desc: Define EvalDataset: JSONL format with question, expected_answer, tags
EvalRunner: run dataset against provider, capture response + latency + tokens
Metrics: exact match, fuzzy match, LLM-as-judge, cost per answer
Snapshot diff: compare current run vs baseline, flag regressions
Output JSONL report for downstream analysis
CLI: `birkin eval run <dataset.jsonl> --provider anthropic`
Tests: eval run produces valid JSONL, regression detection works

acceptance_criteria: Eval runs produce JSONL with latency, tokens, cost per question
Snapshot diff detects regressions
CLI command works end-to-end

tasks.title: Observability logging (structured traces)
tasks.rationale: Structured traces for every turn: provider, tokens, tool calls, latency, outcome. Same data source as Evaluation.

subtasks.desc: Define Trace model: session_id, turn_id, provider, model, tokens_in, tokens_out, tool_calls, latency_ms, outcome
Tracer: context-manager that wraps provider.complete() and tool execution
TraceStorage: append to JSONL file per session, with rotation
Wire tracer into Agent._run_loop and Provider.complete
API: /api/traces?session_id=x for dashboard consumption
Tests: trace emitted on chat, trace contains correct fields

acceptance_criteria: Every agent turn emits a structured trace
Traces include provider, tokens, latency, tool calls
Traces queryable via API

sprints.name: Agent Capabilities
sprints.goal: Computer use, voice, semantic memory — make the agent do real work

tasks.title: Computer Use (Playwright MCP server)
tasks.rationale: Headless browser as MCP server. Unlocks 'monitor → summarize → notify' workflows.

subtasks.desc: Create browser MCP server with Playwright backend
Tools: navigate, screenshot, click, type, extract_text, wait_for_selector
Security: domain allowlist, max page load time, resource limits
Package as SKILL.md (triggers: 'browse', 'screenshot', 'scrape')
Example workflow: daily site check → summary → telegram notification
Tests: navigate + screenshot + extract text round-trip

acceptance_criteria: Browser MCP server starts and exposes tools
Agent can navigate, screenshot, extract text from a page
Domain allowlist enforced

tasks.title: Voice I/O (Whisper STT + TTS)
tasks.rationale: STT input + TTS output. WebUI mic button + Telegram voice messages. Korean-language strength.

subtasks.desc: STT: Whisper API or local whisper.cpp integration
TTS: OpenAI TTS API or local Piper/Coqui
Voice router: POST /api/voice/stt (audio→text), POST /api/voice/tts (text→audio)
WebUI: mic button in chat input, audio playback for TTS responses
Telegram: handle voice messages → STT → agent → TTS → voice reply
Tests: STT produces text from audio, TTS produces audio from text

acceptance_criteria: Voice input transcribed to text via Whisper
Text responses can be played as audio
Telegram voice messages handled end-to-end

tasks.title: Semantic memory (local embeddings)
tasks.rationale: Offline embedding via sentence-transformers/BGE-m3. Zero-dependency semantic search over wiki. Token savings + personalization.

subtasks.desc: EmbeddingEngine: sentence-transformers with BGE-m3 (bilingual KO+EN)
VectorStore: numpy-based cosine similarity (no heavy deps like faiss initially)
Auto-embed wiki pages on ingest, update on edit
Semantic search API: /api/memory/search?q=... returns ranked results
Agent integration: @memory search uses semantic when available, falls back to keyword
Tests: embed, store, retrieve, relevance ranking

acceptance_criteria: Wiki pages auto-embedded on ingest
Semantic search returns relevant results for KO and EN queries
Graceful fallback when model not downloaded

sprints.name: UX & Intelligence
sprints.goal: Dashboard, session fork, NL workflow builder — power-user features

tasks.title: Observability dashboard
tasks.rationale: New WebUI tab: token spend, per-session latency, tool failure rate, cron job history.

subtasks.desc: API: /api/observability/summary (aggregated stats), /api/observability/traces (paginated)
Dashboard page: token spend chart, latency histogram, tool success/failure pie, cron history table
Filter by date range, session, provider
Auto-refresh via SSE or polling
Tests: API returns correct aggregated data

acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows real-time token spend and latency
Drill-down to individual session traces
Cron job history visible

tasks.title: Session fork & replay
tasks.rationale: Branch conversation from any message; re-run from edited past turn. Power-user / prompt-engineering feature.

subtasks.desc: SQLite schema: add parent_session_id, fork_from_seq columns
Session.fork(from_seq): create new session with messages up to seq
Session.replay(from_seq, new_message): re-run from edited point
API: POST /api/sessions/{id}/fork, POST /api/sessions/{id}/replay
WebUI: right-click message → 'Fork from here' / 'Edit & replay'
Tests: fork preserves messages, replay produces different continuation

acceptance_criteria: Fork creates new session with correct message history
Replay re-runs agent from edited message
Parent/child session relationship tracked

tasks.title: Natural language workflow builder
tasks.rationale: Describe automation in a sentence → generate node graph. Depends on stabilized state-graph schema.

subtasks.desc: NLWorkflowBuilder: LLM call that takes description → produces graph JSON
Prompt template with state-graph schema, available node types, examples
API: POST /api/workflows/generate {description} → {workflow_json}
WebUI: text input in workflow editor → 'Generate' button → renders graph
Validation: generated graph must pass schema validation before rendering
Tests: description → valid graph, invalid output → error message

acceptance_criteria: Natural language description generates valid workflow graph
Generated graph renders in workflow editor
Invalid LLM output caught by validation

critical_path: S0 → S1 (MCP) → S2 (State Graph + Triggers) → S4 (Dashboard + NL Builder)
parallel_tracks: S3 (Capabilities) runs parallel to S2 after S1 completes
